incorrects.description: Student added wrong number of rows on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Count how many rows you need.

incorrects.description: Student added wrong number of rows on second attempt

params.description: Row button pulses to draw attention.

steps.text: You're working on it. The problem says 4 rows. Keep pressing the Row + button until you see 4 empty rows.

incorrects.description: Student added wrong number of rows three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System adds rows one at a time until 4 empty row placeholders are visible.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. I press the Row + button 4 times. That gives me 4 empty rows. The answer is 4 rows.

incorrects.description: Student added wrong number of items per row on first attempt

steps.text: Almost. How many dots should go in each row?

incorrects.description: Student added wrong number of items per row on second attempt

params.description: Items per Row button pulses to draw attention.

steps.text: Let's think about this together. The problem says 4 rows of 6. That means 6 dots in every row. Keep pressing Items per Row + until each row has 6 dots.

incorrects.description: Student added wrong number of items per row three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System adds dots one at a time to each row until every row has 6 dots. 24 dots total visible.

steps.text: Here, let me walk you through this one. I press Items per Row + 6 times. Now every row has 6 dots. The answer is 6 items per row.

incorrects.description: Student placed wrong numbers in equation on first attempt

steps.text: Let's try again. Look at the array you built.

incorrects.description: Student placed wrong numbers in equation on second attempt

params.description: Array pulses. Rows and items per row highlight briefly.

steps.text: Here's a hint: count the rows going down. That's your first number. Count the dots across one row. That's your second number. Multiply to find how many dots total.

incorrects.description: Student placed wrong numbers in equation three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System places tiles: 4 in first blank, 6 in second blank, 24 in third blank. Equation reads 4 x 6 = 24.

steps.text: Let me show you how to think about this. The array has 4 rows. That's the first number. Each row has 6 dots. That's the second number. 4 times 6 equals 24. The equation is 4 x 6 = 24.
